query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "macaron-a2ui: a model for generative ui in personal agents" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (e) as an empirical-population canonical study and (a) by investigating the core mechanism of "Human-Agent Alignment" required for generative agents, which is the fundamental prerequisite for extending the "Macaron-A2UI" model of generative UI in personal agents. Although it does not mention the specific "Macaron" architecture, it provides the essential empirical baseline on user expectations and alignment dimensions that any follow-up work on personal agent UI mus
